title: Trump says he 'might have forced Israel's hand' in striking Iran first
fullText: Now PlayingUP NEXTAmericans Stranded in the Middle East as Iran War Disrupts Flights03:11Iran Shuts Down Oil Channel: How It Will Impact the US Economy02:18What to Expect From Next Wave of Potential US Attacks on Iran02:20Opposition to Iran War Grows Among Some Members of Congress02:05Attacks Escalate in Middle East as Iran Retaliates Across Region02:26Iran reports 168 dead in strike on elementary school01:53Can Iran survive a war with the United States?03:44Lebanon emerges as new front in Iran war as Hezbollah trades strikes with Israel03:52Iran War leads to tensions and travel chaos across Middle East01:54Trump says strikes on Iran could last four to five weeks05:15Iran shuts down Strait of Hormuz, oil, and gas prices rise10:05Rubio: Mission is to destroy Iran’s missile capability03:40‘This is an incredible moment’: Iranian-Americans celebrate Khamenei’s death 01:23'We can't find a way home': Americans stranded overseas amid Iran strikes03:48Trump cites failed talks as justification for Iran strikes, but negotiators say otherwise04:23'Whatever it takes': Trump says the Iran conflict will continue05:10Civilians in Middle East on Edge as Iran Launches Retaliatory Strikes02:47What are the military's objectives in Iran?03:55'We'll go as far as we need': Hegseth won't say whether ground troops would be involved in Iran01:13Now PlayingTrump says he 'might have forced Israel's hand' in striking Iran first01:11UP NEXTAmericans Stranded in the Middle East as Iran War Disrupts Flights03:11Iran Shuts Down Oil Channel: How It Will Impact the US Economy02:18What to Expect From Next Wave of Potential US Attacks on Iran02:20Opposition to Iran War Grows Among Some Members of Congress02:05Attacks Escalate in Middle East as Iran Retaliates Across Region02:26
summary: President Donald Trump said he "might have forced Israel's hand" in striking Iran after believing the Iranians would attack first based on progress in negotiations over the nuclear program.
